message:
test(frankenlibc-fuzz): add fuzz_punycode target with seeded corpus

New cargo-fuzz binary under frankenlibc-fuzz covering frankenlibc's
RFC 3492 punycode encode/decode surface. Punycode runs over
arbitrary unicode strings on the encode side and arbitrary ASCII
on the decode side, so the input space is wide and bug-prone in
unusual ways (overflow during the variable-length integer
adaptation, surrogate handling, edge cases at codepoint
boundaries).

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml:
- Wire fuzz_punycode as a [[bin]] alongside fuzz_keyring and the
  other targets, with test=false / doc=false / bench=false to
  match the project's fuzz-target convention.

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_punycode.rs:
- 170 lines covering encode/decode roundtrip, decode panic-clean
  on adversarial input, and the public ABI surface against host
  parity for inputs that should round-trip.

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/corpus/fuzz_punycode/:
- seed_ascii (6 bytes): minimal ASCII seed exercising the
  fast-path branch where no codepoints need encoding.
- seed_delimiter (5 bytes): seed targeting the punycode delimiter
  '-' which marks the boundary between basic and extended
  codepoints — historically the source of off-by-one bugs.
- seed_rfc_chinese (23 bytes): seed from the RFC 3492 sample
  Chinese character set, exercising the multi-codepoint
  variable-length integer adaptation path.

These three seeds are explicitly chosen to bootstrap libfuzzer
toward each branch of the encoder/decoder rather than relying on
random walk to discover them. Subsequent fuzz runs will grow the
corpus with new coverage-driven inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
